summary: The test suite is well-structured and covers all stated requirements: latest for main/master, pr-{number} for PRs, semver tags, branch-sha for feature branches, sanitization rules, and error handling. Organization into named scenario classes with clear Arrange/Act/Assert patterns and meaningful names is excellent. Theory attributes are used appropriately for parametric cases (IsSemverTag with 10 inputs, sanitization rules). The combined scenario test and the special-chars branch test provide good integration coverage. Rigor is strong but not perfect: null/empty BranchName in GenerateTags is not tested (the code silently skips the branch tag); IsDefaultBranch case-insensitivity is not directly tested; there is no test for a tags array containing a mix of semver and non-semver entries to verify the non-semver ones are ignored while semver ones pass. The exact count assertion (Assert.Equal(4, tags.Count)) in the combined test is mildly brittle. Minor deduction for the predicate form of Assert.DoesNotContain having argument order reversed from the standard xUnit convention, though it will still compile and run correctly. Overall a trustworthy suite that would catch most regressions.
